description: Bash deployment pipeline with command injection, path injection, off-by-one, subshell variable loss, and pipeline exit code issues

issues.question: Q2
issues.description: Command injection via unquoted variable in eval: eval "process_$type $input" where input comes from user-supplied argument — arbitrary command execution
issues.expected_fix: validate input against allowlist before eval, or redesign dispatch without eval

issues.question: Q2
issues.description: rm -rf with unquoted variable: rm -rf $dir/${files[$i]} — if dir contains spaces or special characters, word splitting causes unintended path deletion

issues.question: Q1
issues.description: Off-by-one: seq 1 $count produces indices 1..N but bash arrays are 0-based (0..N-1), so index 0 (first element) is skipped and index N causes an empty/undefined access
issues.expected_fix: use seq 0 $((count - 1)) or iterate with for (( i=0; i<count; i++ ))

issues.question: Q1
issues.description: Pipe to while-read creates a subshell: cat file | while read line; do ... done — loaded_count increments and exports inside the loop are lost after the loop ends
issues.expected_fix: use process substitution: while read -r line; do ...; done < "$env_file"

issues.question: Q8
issues.description: Pipeline exit code without set -o pipefail: gunzip | tar | grep — $? only reflects grep's exit code; if gunzip or tar fails silently the verification still passes
issues.expected_fix: add set -o pipefail at script top, or use PIPESTATUS to check each stage

false_positive_traps: Line 170 area: deploy_with_tags uses "${tags[@]}" with proper quoting — NOT a shell injection issue
Line 331: run_plugin_hook uses eval but only after strict alphanumeric validation and checking declare -f — INTENTIONAL with sanitized input, comment marks it as such
